2603.19504.title: Beyond the Desk: Barriers and Future Opportunities for AI to Assist Scientists in Embodied Physical Tasks
2603.19504.paper_summary: More scientists are now using AI, but prior studies have examined only how they use it 'at the desk' for computer-based work. However, given that scientific work often happens 'beyond the desk' at lab and field sites, we conducted the first study of how scientific practitioners use AI for embodied physical tasks. We interviewed 12 scientific practitioners doing hands-on lab and fieldwork in domains like nuclear fusion, primate cognition, and biochemistry, and found three barriers to AI adoption in these settings: 1) experimental setups are too high-stakes to risk AI errors, 2) constrained environments make it hard to use AI, and 3) AI cannot match the tacit knowledge of humans. Participants then developed speculative designs for future AI assistants to 1) monitor task status, 2) organize lab-wide knowledge, 3) monitor scientists' health, 4) do field scouting, 5) do hands-on chores. Our findings point toward AI as background infrastructure to support physical work rather than replacing human expertise.
2603.19504.paper_summary_zh: 越來越多的科學家現在正在使用人工智慧，但之前的研究僅考察了他們如何在「桌面」上進行基於電腦的工作。<lb/>然而，考慮到科學工作通常在實驗室和現場進行「超越桌面」，我們進行了首次研究，探討科學從業者如何將人工智慧應用於具體的實體任務。<lb/>我們訪問了12位在核融合、靈長類認知和生物化學等領域從事實驗室和現場工作的科學從業者，並發現了這些環境中人工智慧採用的三個障礙：1）實驗設置的風險太高，不敢冒人工智慧出錯的風險，2）受限的環境使得使用人工智慧變得困難，3）人工智慧無法匹敵人類的隱性知識。<lb/>參與者隨後為未來的人工智慧助手開發了推測性設計，以1）監控任務狀態，2）組織實驗室內的知識，3）監控科學家的健康，4）進行現場勘查，5）執行實際的雜務。<lb/>我們的研究結果表明，人工智慧應作為支持實體工作的背景基礎設施，而不是取代人類專業知識。
2603.19504.author: Irene Hou et.al.
2603.19504.authors: Irene Hou, Alexander Qin, Lauren Cheng, Philip J. Guo
